description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the image registry.gitlab.com/omicronns.main/cpp-project-template-docker with three stages: test, export, and build. Define a global before_script that exports PROJECT_NAME, CONAN_USER_HOME, CONAN_PACKAGE_VERSION, CONAN_PACKAGE_CHANNEL, CONAN_REFERENCE, PYTHON and clones the conan-home repository, and a global after_script that re‑exports CONAN_USER_HOME and removes all Conan packages. Add a test job in the test stage that runs the commands: conan install -if build . -e build=true, conan build -bf build ., and build/bin/$PROJECT_NAME-test, using the docker tag. Add an export job in the export stage that runs only on branches matching /^stable-.*$/ or /^testing-.*$/ and executes: conan export . $CONAN_LOGIN_USERNAME/$CONAN_PACKAGE_CHANNEL and conan upload $CONAN_REFERENCE $CONAN_UPLOAD_CONFIG --confirm --force, also using the docker tag. Add a gcc7_x86_64 job in the build stage that runs only on the same branch patterns, sets CONAN_BUILD_CONFIG to --profile gcc7_x86_64, and runs: conan install . $CONAN_REFERENCE $CONAN_BUILD_CONFIG --build=$PROJECT_NAME --build=outdated -e build=true followed by conan upload $CONAN_REFERENCE $CONAN_UPLOAD_CONFIG --confirm --force --all, again using the docker tag.
